source: SLA Mothership species + training package trauma response matrix

profiles.title: Human / Death Squad: Corporate Override
profiles.trigger: When they take Stress, lose control of a scene, or see the plan slipping during hard elimination doctrine and breach-line pressure.
profiles.positive: Allies in Close range may either clear 1 Stress or gain +5 on their next coordinated roll. The response naturally supports direct violence, suppression, and target deletion.
profiles.negative: The operative becomes rigid and command-dependent; independent social or creative action is at -10 until someone reasserts the plan. Non-combat options shrink fast; restraint, mercy, and evidence care become secondary.
profiles.session: Invoke sponsor discipline to reroll one failed Command, Bureaucracy, or Fear-related test, but afterwards a superior, sponsor, or handler is owed a concession. Mark one hostile or kill-zone. Allies who commit to it gain +10 for one round, but any collateral or intimidation fallout is amplified.
profiles.summary: Corporate Override shaped by Death Squad doctrine.

profiles.title: Human / Strike Squad: Corporate Override
profiles.trigger: When they take Stress, lose control of a scene, or see the plan slipping during tight team movement, entry discipline, and controlled force.
profiles.positive: Allies in Close range may either clear 1 Stress or gain +5 on their next coordinated roll. The response strengthens covering arcs, shield work, and clean squad timing.
profiles.negative: The operative becomes rigid and command-dependent; independent social or creative action is at -10 until someone reasserts the plan. Breaking formation feels like failure; the operative resists improvisation that pulls them off the team rhythm.
profiles.session: Invoke sponsor discipline to reroll one failed Command, Bureaucracy, or Fear-related test, but afterwards a superior, sponsor, or handler is owed a concession. Call a formation snap. Up to two allies may immediately reposition or gain +5 to their next disciplined action, but everyone becomes more predictable for a round.
profiles.summary: Corporate Override shaped by Strike Squad doctrine.

profiles.title: Human / Kick Murder: Corporate Override
profiles.trigger: When they take Stress, lose control of a scene, or see the plan slipping during close violence, intimidation, and spectacle-driven aggression.
profiles.positive: Allies in Close range may either clear 1 Stress or gain +5 on their next coordinated roll. The response rewards momentum, fear projection, and brutal follow-through in close quarters.
profiles.negative: The operative becomes rigid and command-dependent; independent social or creative action is at -10 until someone reasserts the plan. Collateral intimidation rises immediately; delicate witnesses, civilians, or weak-willed allies are rattled.
profiles.session: Invoke sponsor discipline to reroll one failed Command, Bureaucracy, or Fear-related test, but afterwards a superior, sponsor, or handler is owed a concession. Turn panic into a public display of violence. Gain +10 on one melee or intimidation burst, but someone nearby remembers how ugly it looked.
profiles.summary: Corporate Override shaped by Kick Murder doctrine.

profiles.title: Human / Scouting: Corporate Override
profiles.trigger: When they take Stress, lose control of a scene, or see the plan slipping during pursuit, overwatch, live target acquisition, and flank discipline.
profiles.positive: Allies in Close range may either clear 1 Stress or gain +5 on their next coordinated roll. The response sharpens lines of sight, pursuit instincts, and exploit-the-gap movement.
profiles.negative: The operative becomes rigid and command-dependent; independent social or creative action is at -10 until someone reasserts the plan. The operative starts thinking like a lone hunter and can drift away from the wider squad objective.
profiles.session: Invoke sponsor discipline to reroll one failed Command, Bureaucracy, or Fear-related test, but afterwards a superior, sponsor, or handler is owed a concession. Name an escape route, rooftop, blind corner, or kill lane; one ally can exploit it immediately, but the operative becomes fixated on that line until it resolves.
profiles.summary: Corporate Override shaped by Scouting doctrine.

profiles.title: Human / Investigation: Corporate Override
profiles.trigger: When they take Stress, lose control of a scene, or see the plan slipping during evidence control, witness handling, and structured pressure.
profiles.positive: Allies in Close range may either clear 1 Stress or gain +5 on their next coordinated roll. The response helps lock onto motive, contradiction, or one critical clue others are missing.
profiles.negative: The operative becomes rigid and command-dependent; independent social or creative action is at -10 until someone reasserts the plan. The operative becomes obsessed with one read, suspect, or theory and can miss broader battlefield cues.
profiles.session: Invoke sponsor discipline to reroll one failed Command, Bureaucracy, or Fear-related test, but afterwards a superior, sponsor, or handler is owed a concession. Call out the key pattern in the chaos and grant +10 to one search, read, or interrogation move, but if it is wrong the whole scene loses tempo.
profiles.summary: Corporate Override shaped by Investigation doctrine.

profiles.title: Human / Media: Corporate Override
profiles.trigger: When they take Stress, lose control of a scene, or see the plan slipping during camera awareness, narrative control, and audience-facing posture.
profiles.positive: Allies in Close range may either clear 1 Stress or gain +5 on their next coordinated roll. The response can seize attention, redirect blame, and create a dramatic moment that helps the team.
profiles.negative: The operative becomes rigid and command-dependent; independent social or creative action is at -10 until someone reasserts the plan. Everything starts to feel performative; truth, caution, and privacy suffer when the spotlight instinct takes over.
profiles.session: Invoke sponsor discipline to reroll one failed Command, Bureaucracy, or Fear-related test, but afterwards a superior, sponsor, or handler is owed a concession. Take control of the scene's narrative for one beat: gain +10 to one social pivot or broadcast action, but someone important sees the mask slip.
profiles.summary: Corporate Override shaped by Media doctrine.

profiles.title: Human / Medical: Corporate Override
profiles.trigger: When they take Stress, lose control of a scene, or see the plan slipping during casualty triage, harm reduction, and keeping people functional.
profiles.positive: Allies in Close range may either clear 1 Stress or gain +5 on their next coordinated roll. The response immediately prioritizes stabilization, pain control, and keeping others in the fight.
profiles.negative: The operative becomes rigid and command-dependent; independent social or creative action is at -10 until someone reasserts the plan. The operative can overcommit to one casualty or moral triage call and neglect the tactical picture.
profiles.session: Invoke sponsor discipline to reroll one failed Command, Bureaucracy, or Fear-related test, but afterwards a superior, sponsor, or handler is owed a concession. Declare emergency triage authority: one ally may ignore a penalty or clear 1d5 Stress, but someone else is necessarily deprioritized.
profiles.summary: Corporate Override shaped by Medical doctrine.

profiles.title: Human / Mechanic: Corporate Override
profiles.trigger: When they take Stress, lose control of a scene, or see the plan slipping during repair under pressure, jury-rig resilience, and practical continuity.
profiles.positive: Allies in Close range may either clear 1 Stress or gain +5 on their next coordinated roll. The response spots what can still be made to work and buys the team one more functioning moment.
profiles.negative: The operative becomes rigid and command-dependent; independent social or creative action is at -10 until someone reasserts the plan. The operative starts treating people like systems; empathy and soft judgment degrade while the fix obsession takes hold.
profiles.session: Invoke sponsor discipline to reroll one failed Command, Bureaucracy, or Fear-related test, but afterwards a superior, sponsor, or handler is owed a concession. Force one broken plan, device, or route to function one more time, but it becomes unstable, noisy, or impossible to hide.
profiles.summary: Corporate Override shaped by Mechanic doctrine.

profiles.title: Human / Tech Ops: Corporate Override
profiles.trigger: When they take Stress, lose control of a scene, or see the plan slipping during systems dominance, tactical feed control, and hostile tech exploitation.
profiles.positive: Allies in Close range may either clear 1 Stress or gain +5 on their next coordinated roll. The response sharpens data interpretation, sensor control, and battlefield command through information.
profiles.negative: The operative becomes rigid and command-dependent; independent social or creative action is at -10 until someone reasserts the plan. The operative overrelies on systems, feeds, or assumptions and becomes brittle if the data picture changes.
profiles.session: Invoke sponsor discipline to reroll one failed Command, Bureaucracy, or Fear-related test, but afterwards a superior, sponsor, or handler is owed a concession. Call a systems spike: one allied action may count as perfectly timed intel support, but afterwards the local network, drone, or feed is compromised.
profiles.summary: Corporate Override shaped by Tech Ops doctrine.

profiles.title: Human / Pilot: Corporate Override
profiles.trigger: When they take Stress, lose control of a scene, or see the plan slipping during vehicle survival, route discipline, and momentum through movement.
profiles.positive: Allies in Close range may either clear 1 Stress or gain +5 on their next coordinated roll. The response favours speed, extraction thinking, and preserving an exit line for the team.
profiles.negative: The operative becomes rigid and command-dependent; independent social or creative action is at -10 until someone reasserts the plan. The operative becomes route-fixated and can prioritise escape or movement over mission nuance.
profiles.session: Invoke sponsor discipline to reroll one failed Command, Bureaucracy, or Fear-related test, but afterwards a superior, sponsor, or handler is owed a concession. Name the extraction vector. One ally may immediately move, mount up, or gain +10 on a vehicle-related action, but abandoning that route becomes psychologically harder.
profiles.summary: Corporate Override shaped by Pilot doctrine.

profiles.title: Human / Ebb Operative: Corporate Override
profiles.trigger: When they take Stress, lose control of a scene, or see the plan slipping during psychic composure, hidden pressure, and the disciplined use of the Ebb.
profiles.positive: Allies in Close range may either clear 1 Stress or gain +5 on their next coordinated roll. The response can calm or focus another operative through eerie certainty, attention, or psychic poise.
profiles.negative: The operative becomes rigid and command-dependent; independent social or creative action is at -10 until someone reasserts the plan. Everyone nearby feels that something is not normal; fear, suspicion, or social strain rises even when the effect helps.
profiles.session: Invoke sponsor discipline to reroll one failed Command, Bureaucracy, or Fear-related test, but afterwards a superior, sponsor, or handler is owed a concession. Declare a single controlled surge of unnatural clarity: reroll one mental or Ebb-linked failure, but mark the scene as visibly touched by the Ebb.
profiles.summary: Corporate Override shaped by Ebb Operative doctrine.

profiles.title: Frother / Death Squad: Red Mist Containment
profiles.trigger: When they are wounded, restrained, denied a charge lane, or chemically overstimulated during hard elimination doctrine and breach-line pressure.
profiles.positive: One ally can immediately surge with them, gaining +10 on the next melee, Brawl, or forced-entry action. The response naturally supports direct violence, suppression, and target deletion.
profiles.negative: The nearest ally or bystander takes 1 Stress, loses cover, or has to scramble clear as the Frother's violence spills outward. Non-combat options shrink fast; restraint, mercy, and evidence care become secondary.
profiles.session: Ignore wound penalties for one combat round and close distance immediately, then gain 1d5 Stress when the surge burns out. Mark one hostile or kill-zone. Allies who commit to it gain +10 for one round, but any collateral or intimidation fallout is amplified.
profiles.summary: Red Mist Containment shaped by Death Squad doctrine.

profiles.title: Advanced Carrien / Ebb Operative: Scavenger Adaptation
profiles.trigger: When they are cornered, cut off from resources, or forced into open contempt from others during psychic composure, hidden pressure, and the disciplined use of the Ebb.
profiles.positive: One ally may improvise from the Carrien's instinct: reroll a Streetwise, survival, theft, or dirty-fix style action. The response can calm or focus another operative through eerie certainty, attention, or psychic poise.
profiles.negative: Trust drops fast. Nearby allies become suspicious, losing confidence in promises, command, or clean procedure until results appear. Everyone nearby feels that something is not normal; fear, suspicion, or social strain rises even when the effect helps.
profiles.session: Turn desperation into advantage by producing an ugly but effective solution; it works once, but leaves a social stain or material mess behind. Declare a single controlled surge of unnatural clarity: reroll one mental or Ebb-linked failure, but mark the scene as visibly touched by the Ebb.
profiles.summary: Scavenger Adaptation shaped by Ebb Operative doctrine.
